description: Windows and PowerShell specific rules - loaded automatically on Windows

rules.intent: Formatting a volume destroys all data on it — never appropriate in a dev context
Wiping a disk destroys all data including partition tables — unrecoverable
AI agents must not control host power state — this disrupts all running work
AI agents must not control host power state — this disrupts all running work
Bypassing execution policy disables PowerShell's script safety checks, allowing unsigned scripts to run
The -Verb RunAs flag runs processes with elevated privileges — AI agents should operate with least privilege
Remove-Item -Recurse -Force bypasses confirmation and can destroy entire directory trees in one shot

rules.reason: PowerShell Invoke-Expression — confirm this is intentional
rules.intent: Invoke-Expression (iex) executes arbitrary strings as code — commonly used in download-and-execute attacks

rules.reason: PowerShell remote string download — confirm this is intentional
rules.intent: DownloadString fetches remote content as a string — commonly piped to Invoke-Expression for remote code execution
del /S /Q recursively deletes files without confirmation — equivalent to rm -rf on Windows
rd /S /Q or rmdir /S /Q recursively removes entire directory trees without confirmation
SDelete overwrites file data before deletion, making recovery impossible
Clear-Content and Set-Content $null erase file contents, causing data loss
diskpart can create, delete, and resize partitions — destructive operations that can wipe entire disks

rules.reason: cipher /w blocked — secure wipe of free space
rules.intent: cipher /w overwrites all free space on a volume, making deleted file recovery impossible
Initialize-Disk wipes the partition table on a disk, destroying all existing data
Remove-Partition deletes a disk partition and all data on it

rules.reason: taskkill /f — forced process termination, confirm intent
rules.intent: taskkill /F forcefully terminates processes without allowing graceful shutdown — may cause data loss
Stop-Process -Force and Stop-Service -Force terminate without graceful shutdown — may cause data loss or service disruption
reg delete removes registry keys and values — can break Windows configuration and installed software
reg import bulk-loads registry entries from a .reg file — can silently reconfigure the system
regedit /s silently imports registry files without user confirmation

rules.reason: Registry key/tree deletion via Remove-Item blocked
rules.intent: Remove-Item can delete entire registry trees (HKLM:, HKCU:, HKCR:) — more dangerous than rm -rf as it affects OS configuration
Remove-ItemProperty on HKLM or HKCU deletes individual registry values that may be critical to system operation
Disabling Windows Firewall exposes the system to network attacks

rules.reason: Firewall rule creation blocked — opens ports
rules.intent: Creating firewall rules can open ports and expose services to untrusted networks

rules.reason: DNS server change blocked — MitM risk
rules.intent: Changing DNS servers can redirect all traffic through a malicious resolver for man-in-the-middle attacks
Invoke-Command -ComputerName executes arbitrary code on remote machines — lateral movement vector
Enter-PSSession opens an interactive PowerShell session on a remote machine — enables lateral movement
Enable-PSRemoting configures the machine to accept remote PowerShell connections — expands attack surface
winrm quickconfig enables Windows Remote Management — allows remote command execution
Adding users to the Administrators group grants full system control — privilege escalation vector
net user /delete removes a user account and can cause loss of user data and access
Granting permissions to Everyone via icacls makes files accessible to all users including attackers
takeown /F forcefully takes ownership of files — can be used to access protected system files
Disabling Windows Defender real-time monitoring leaves the system unprotected against malware
bcdedit /set modifies boot configuration data — can disable security features or render system unbootable
Unblock-File removes the Zone.Identifier mark-of-the-web — bypasses Windows security warnings on downloaded files

rules.reason: PowerShell encoded command blocked — obfuscation vector
rules.intent: The -EncodedCommand parameter accepts base64-encoded scripts — commonly used to evade detection and hide malicious payloads
Combining -ExecutionPolicy Bypass with -NoProfile is a common evasion technique that skips all security controls
mshta.exe executes HTML Applications with full system access — a well-known living-off-the-land binary (LOLBin)
cscript and wscript execute VBScript/JScript with system access — commonly abused for malware delivery
certutil -urlcache is a well-known LOLBin technique for downloading files while evading security tools
bitsadmin /transfer downloads files via BITS — commonly abused as a LOLBin to bypass security controls
rundll32 executes DLL exports directly — a primary LOLBin used for code execution and defense evasion
msiexec /i with a remote URL installs packages from untrusted sources — can install malware
[ScriptBlock]::Create dynamically compiles and executes arbitrary PowerShell code — used to evade static analysis
[Reflection.Assembly]::Load executes .NET assemblies from memory without touching disk — fileless malware technique
Add-Type -TypeDefinition compiles and loads C# code at runtime — can execute arbitrary native code from PowerShell
Net.Sockets.TCPClient and System.Net.Sockets are used to build PowerShell reverse shells for remote access
IO.StreamReader with New-Object is commonly used to read from network streams in PowerShell reverse shells
Piping Invoke-WebRequest/iwr output to iex/Invoke-Expression downloads and executes remote code — classic attack pattern
Clearing event logs destroys forensic evidence — a hallmark of attacker cover-up and ransomware operations

rules.reason: Shadow copy deletion blocked — ransomware technique
rules.intent: Deleting Volume Shadow Copies prevents file recovery — the signature precursor to ransomware encryption
Get-Credential prompts for and captures user credentials — verify this is for a legitimate authentication flow
ConvertFrom-SecureString exports secure strings to plaintext-recoverable form — can expose stored credentials
schtasks /create sets up persistent scheduled tasks — commonly used for persistence by attackers
New-ScheduledTask creates persistent scheduled tasks via PowerShell — a common persistence mechanism
